bead.title: ddx work: wedge guard counts only true wedges at BOTH sites + clears marker on un-park (D3)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Two defects in the consecutive-wedge guard (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go):
(1) The guard counts wedges at TWO sites but D-scope only addressed one — recordConsecutiveWedge is called at the progress_watchdog path AND the route-resolution-timeout path (search recordConsecutiveWedge; sites ~5751 and ~5634 in the pre-D1 tree, re-pin against current HEAD). A slow-but-fine route resolution can still trip the threshold-2 guard and park a healthy bead.
(2) Un-parking a wedge-parked bead does NOT clear the marker: setting status=open (or the operator un-parking) leaves the work-consecutive-wedges custom field (key executeLoopWedgeMarkerKey="work-consecutive-wedges") at count>=threshold, so the worker re-parks the bead on the next claim WITHOUT attempting it. Observed 2026-06-02: 5 beads un-parked via --status open were immediately re-parked; operator had to --unset work-consecutive-wedges manually. clearConsecutiveWedge exists (~5844) and is called on clean completion (~2604) but never on un-park/reopen.

ROOT CAUSE
consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips(readWedgeMarker(candidate.Extra), threshold) at ~1745 trips before any attempt when the marker count>=threshold. Nothing clears the marker when a bead transitions proposed/blocked->open by operator action.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Gate BOTH recordConsecutiveWedge call sites on genuine no-progress: only increment when the heartbeat did not advance within the budget window (the watchdog/route-timeout fired on a true stall), not on every watchdog/timeout. After D1 records the route, the progress_watchdog site rarely fires; the route-timeout site must also not count a slow-but-successful resolution.
2. Clear the wedge marker when a bead is reopened to an execution-eligible state by operator action: on the open-transition path (or when the loop first re-encounters a previously-parked bead that is now status=open with a stale marker AND its blocking condition is gone), call clearConsecutiveWedge so the bead gets a fresh attempt instead of an instant re-park. Equivalently, consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips must not trip on a marker whose bead was explicitly reopened.

NON-SCOPE: D1 (route recording) is merged. Do not change phaseEmpty or the watchdog budget.

ACCEPTANCE
1. New test: flagWedgedForOperatorAttention / the route-timeout reporter does NOT increment work-consecutive-wedges when lastActivityAt advanced within the budget (heartbeat progressed).
2. New test: a bead with work-consecutive-wedges count>=threshold that is reopened (status open via operator) is claimable and attempted (consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips returns false after reopen / the marker is cleared), not instantly re-parked.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
